steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to provide an example of a sky island. The answer must name a specific sky island — not define the concept, quote descriptions of sky islands in general, or list how many exist without naming one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Sky islands are isolated mountain ranges whose environments differ drastically… 'islands' surrounded by deserts": defines and describes sky islands using a USFS quote; names no specific example. ✗
  • Choice B — "The USFS considers each of the sky islands… to be a 'unique ecosystem'": quotes the USFS on sky islands in general; still no specific named example. ✗
  • Choice C — "There are dozens of sky islands… such as the El Pinito Mountains.": names the El Pinito Mountains as a concrete example using "such as." ✓
  • Choice D — "In the southwestern US and northwestern Mexico, there are dozens of sky islands: isolated mountain ranges…": gives a count and a definition but does not name any specific sky island. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
